overview.summary: {{REPO_NAME}} is a {{LEVEL}}-friendly service in the platform. This onboarding artifact walks you from zero to confident contributor.
overview.purpose: Provide a reliable, observable, and secure surface area for downstream consumers while keeping developer ergonomics high.
keyFeatures: Modular service architecture with clean boundaries
First-class observability: metrics, tracing, structured logs
Comprehensive automated test coverage (unit, integration, e2e)

architecture.description: Requests enter through an API Gateway, are authenticated against the Identity service, then routed to domain services. State lives in a Postgres primary with Redis for hot caches; async work goes through an event bus.

envSetup.description: Redis connection string used for caches.
Public key used to verify Identity-issued JWTs.
Feature flag to enable new processing pipeline.

buildCommands.label: Install dependencies
buildCommands.description: Installs all runtime and dev dependencies.

buildCommands.label: Run locally
buildCommands.description: Starts the dev server with hot reload.

buildCommands.label: Run tests
buildCommands.description: Executes the full automated test suite.

buildCommands.label: Lint & type-check
buildCommands.description: Runs lint, format and typecheck in one go.

deployment.description: CI builds an immutable container image and signs the SBOM.
Static analysis + dependency scan + secret scan.
Auto-deploy to staging cluster on merge to main.

commonIssues.title: Port already in use
commonIssues.resolution: Kill the previous process: `lsof -i :8080 | xargs kill -9` or change PORT.

commonIssues.title: JWT verification failing
commonIssues.resolution: Ensure JWT_PUBLIC_KEY matches the active Identity signing key. Pull latest from secrets manager.

commonIssues.title: Slow DB queries
commonIssues.resolution: Run `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` on the failing query; check that index on (tenant_id, created_at) exists.

commonIssues.title: Redis connection refused
commonIssues.resolution: Verify REDIS_URL and that the sidecar is healthy via `kubectl describe pod`.

faqs.q: Where do I start as a new joiner?
faqs.a: Pair with your buddy, run the service locally, then pick an issue tagged ‘good first issue’.

faqs.q: How do I get DB access?
faqs.a: Request short-lived credentials via the Vault Identity self-serve portal.

faqs.q: Which tests are required before merging?
faqs.a: Unit + integration must pass. E2E runs nightly and on release branches.

faqs.q: How do I propose an architecture change?
faqs.a: Open an RFC in the docs repo and tag the platform leads for review.

faqs.q: Who do I contact for prod incidents?
faqs.a: Use the on-call rotation in PagerDuty; the escalation matrix is in the Team page.

codeWalkthrough.title: App entry point
codeWalkthrough.explanation: We load config first (env + secrets), construct the HTTP server with all dependencies wired, and start listening. Fatal errors crash fast so the orchestrator can restart us cleanly.

codeWalkthrough.title: Domain entity
codeWalkthrough.explanation: Plain-old class that captures invariants and behavior. No framework imports here — that’s on purpose so we can unit-test it in isolation.

codeWalkthrough.title: Route handler
codeWalkthrough.explanation: Validation, authz, service call, response. Notice we never reach into the DB from here — controllers stay thin.

glossary.definition: An operation that produces the same result if executed multiple times with the same input.
Rolling a new version to a small slice of traffic and watching SLOs before going wider.
Service Level Objective — the reliability target we commit to (e.g. 99.9% successful requests).
Request For Comments — a written proposal inviting feedback before implementation.
Operational model where Git is the source of truth for desired infra state.

learningPath.title: Get the repo running locally
learningPath.description: Clone, install, set up .env, run `npm run dev`, and hit /v1/health.

learningPath.title: Read the architecture diagram
learningPath.description: Understand request flow, data ownership, and async boundaries.

learningPath.title: Tour the folder structure
learningPath.description: Open each top-level folder; map intent to code.

learningPath.title: Follow one request end-to-end
learningPath.description: Pick GET /v1/resources/:id and trace it from controller to DB.

learningPath.title: Run the test suite
learningPath.description: Unit + integration. Add a failing test, watch it fail, then fix.

learningPath.title: Read the on-call runbook
learningPath.description: Know what to do at 3am: dashboards, runbooks, escalation.

learningPath.title: Meet your SMEs
learningPath.description: 15-min intros with Tech Lead, Backend SME, and DevOps SME.

learningPath.title: Ship a tiny PR
learningPath.description: Pick a ‘good first issue’ label, open a PR, get a review.
